methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $169M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.5B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
CarbonCure Technologies is a Series F climate and energy company that has raised $169M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), focused on same-sector and same/adjacent-stage positioning within frontier climate technology.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $169M total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a typical 1.3x step-up multiplier for Series F advancement, implying a valuation of $220M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Helion (Series F, $5.4B), TAE Technologies (Series G, $6.0B), and Form Energy (Series G, $3.5B). The median valuation across this set is $5.4B, with a range of $3.5B to $6.0B.

These methods produce a wide triangulated range of $187M to $6.2B, with a median of $2.8B. The substantial dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty in how to weight stage-progression mechanics—which ground valuation in capital efficiency and historical raise patterns—against peer comparables, where valuations reflect market-cleared prices for later-stage, often better-capitalized competitors. Key uncertainties include CarbonCure's revenue trajectory relative to peers, capital efficiency, path to cash flow, and whether peer multiples sustain. The peer-median approach likely represents the most market-disciplined anchor given recent close comparables.
